title: ScrollArea Component
content:
The `ScrollArea` component provides a customizable, native-like scrolling experience for content that exceeds the viewport. It's built on top of `@radix-ui/react-scroll-area` and offers a clean, accessible way to handle overflow. ## Key Exports - **`ScrollArea`**: The main component for creating scrollable areas. It accepts standard `div` props and props from `ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root`. - **`ScrollBar`**: A component to customize the appearance and behavior of the scrollbars within the `ScrollArea`. It accepts props from `ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar`. ## `ScrollArea` Props Accepts all props from `@radix-ui/react-scroll-area`'s `Root` component, including: - `className`: Custom CSS classes for styling the scroll area container. - `children`: The content to be displayed within the scrollable area. ## `ScrollBar` Props Accepts all props from `@radix-ui/react-scroll-area`'s `ScrollAreaScrollbar` component, including: - `className`: Custom CSS classes for styling the scrollbar. - `orientation`: Controls the orientation of the scrollbar ('vertical' or 'horizontal'). Defaults to 'vertical'. ## Usage Example ```tsx import { ScrollArea } from '@/components/ui/scroll-area'; function MyComponent() { return (

))}
); } ``` This example creates a `ScrollArea` with a fixed height and width, a border, and some sample content. When the content overflows, vertical and horizontal scrollbars will appear automatically.
